lastReleaseMessage: Dashboard + backlog reconciled to reality; maintenance pass. Confirmed M1 survival loop already shipped (waves, oil-zero fail, fog, save/load, win/lose, convoy) and marked DONE; corrected stale gates; cut not-on-us rows (Navy art, Discord URL, superseded auto-worker slider); dashboard counts only active-milestone open rows; untracked *.ps1.bak, deleted orphan source/world stubs, fixed building.gd header, recorded Spine version lock + scoped macOS/Linux out, started OPTIMIZATION_LOG.md; br-bp items tracked in the adherence doc.

barRaise.topFinding: This is the cycle that cleared the board. One commit landed since the 06-23 review (v0.59.4, a reconciliation and maintenance pass) and it closed most of the standing action list at once: .ps1.bak untracked, the dead source/world/ stubs deleted, building.gd's phantom-version header corrected, OPTIMIZATION_LOG.md started, the Spine version lock recorded, and the macOS/Linux gap retired by the owner's Windows-only M4 descope. More important, the 'M1 stalled at 40%' narrative that held the last four verdicts was a stale-dashboard artifact: the survival mechanics are real code (save_load.gd, mission.gd, oil-zero fail, Hold-the-Wall win/lose), shipped in earlier cycles and confirmed at 50% this pass. Zero HIGH findings, clean fast pipe. The two watch items left are infra: the durable release gate is unbuilt, and the work-queue drain that stranded BR's drift ids on 06-22 is not yet truncation-safe.

actions.title: Land br-release-footer-and-automsg-gate-2026-06-18 (two release.ps1 preship guards); tenth audit, the durable closer for the GDD-footer and bare-CHANGELOG drift classes.
Make the YaE .work-queue.json drain truncation-safe (rotate _drain_log to a sidecar) so a FUSE truncation cannot strand the items array again; it dropped BR's four drift ids on 06-22.
